top_signals.title: Bank of England launches policy statement and draft rules on regulating systemic stablecoins
top_signals.summary: The Bank of England has today published its policy statement and draft Code of Practice (rules) for systemic stablecoin issuers.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: US and Iran make ‘progress’ in talks
top_signals.summary: Tehran and Washington locked in negotiations in Switzerland over deal to settle four-month conflict
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Too Big To Break Again: India, Italy, and the Defense Partnership That Almost Wasn’t
top_signals.summary:
A single bribe nearly ruined a defense partnership most people didn’t know even existed. It took India and Italy almost a decade to recover.The story of how that rupture happened — and what it exposed about Italy’s quiet but deep role in India’s military — is essential to understanding why both countries now treat their renewed ties as something too valuable to lose again.When Indian authorities cancelled the AgustaWestland helicopter contract in 2014, the fallout went far beyond the headlines. Torpedo supplies to the Indian Navy’s Scorpène-class submarines froze, upgrades to over 20 Sea King helicopters stalled, and a naval

The post Too Big To Break Again: India, Italy, and the Defense Partnership That Almost Wasn’t appeared first on War on the Rocks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: European Gas Edges Higher After Rocky Start to Iran Peace Talks
top_signals.summary: European natural gas edged higher after talks between the US and Iran appeared to get off to a shaky start, with President Donald Trump issuing fresh threats even as discussions on a peace deal proceed.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: ‘A little funky’: Japanese ‘nattō’ disgusts and delights the world
top_signals.summary: Aside from its supposed health benefits, 'nattō' has long been loved in Japan for its stunning affordability — charms the rest of the world seems to be warming up to.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: South Africa Deploys Police As Anti-Migrant Deadline Looms
top_signals.summary: South Africa will deploy more police this week to deter possible violence before a June 30 deadline set by xenophobic protest groups for foreigners to leave the country.
